Warmup__Z1-Z2__45-75.DARK_HUMOR: Your legs are waking up — give them something to wake up to. These next few minutes set the tone for everything that follows.
Warmup__Z1-Z2__45-75.MOTIVATIONAL: Great start! You're setting the foundation for an amazing workout. Every pedal stroke is preparing your body for the challenge ahead.
Warmup__Z1-Z2__45-75.TECHNICAL: Gradually elevating core temperature and muscle activation. This progressive warm-up increases blood flow and prepares neuromuscular systems for harder efforts.
Warmup__Z1-Z2__45-75.MIXED: Take your time here. You're building momentum, both physically and mentally. A solid warm-up is the difference between a good workout and a great one.

Recovery__Z1__40-55.DARK_HUMOR: This is where you pretend to do something. Easy spinning, light breathing, zero suffering. Your legs needed this more than you realize.
Recovery__Z1__40-55.MOTIVATIONAL: You're giving your body exactly what it needs to bounce back stronger. This recovery work is part of your growth — respect the process.
Recovery__Z1__40-55.TECHNICAL: Low-intensity work promotes parasympathetic activation and lactate clearance without systemic fatigue. Critical for adaptation and long-term progression.
Recovery__Z1__40-55.MIXED: Dial it way back. This isn't about effort — it's about listening to your body. Easy breathing, easy legs, easy mind.

Easy Spin__Z1__35-50.DARK_HUMOR: Just spin easy and recover. Your legs earned this peace.
Easy Spin__Z1__35-50.MOTIVATIONAL: Active recovery is where champions build resilience. Easy spins keep your aerobic engine purring without breaking it down.
Easy Spin__Z1__35-50.TECHNICAL: Z1 active recovery promotes blood flow and metabolic waste clearance while keeping HR below 50% of max. Optimize daily recovery.
Easy Spin__Z1__35-50.MIXED: Spin smooth and easy. Your body is still learning and adapting from yesterday's hard work.

Endurance__Z2__55-75.DARK_HUMOR: Welcome to the sweet spot of not dying. You can hold a conversation here, which is both a blessing and a curse because you have 90 minutes to think about life.
Endurance__Z2__55-75.MOTIVATIONAL: This is where champions are built. Long, steady efforts like this teach your body to burn fat efficiently and build an aerobic foundation that lasts.
Endurance__Z2__55-75.TECHNICAL: Zone 2 aerobic work maximizes fat oxidation and mitochondrial density while staying below lactate threshold. This is the most efficient training stimulus for endurance adaptations.
Endurance__Z2__55-75.MIXED: Stay steady. Your breathing should be controlled but present. This is the bread and butter of cycling fitness — consistent, sustainable, powerful.

Steady Endurance__Z2__56-76.DARK_HUMOR: Long and steady wins races. Keep pedaling, keep breathing, don't overcomplicate it.
Steady Endurance__Z2__56-76.MOTIVATIONAL: Every minute here builds your aerobic engine. You're becoming more efficient, more capable, more powerful with each rotation.
Steady Endurance__Z2__56-76.TECHNICAL: Sustained Z2 work develops aerobic capacity and fat-burning efficiency. This is the foundation all hard work rests on.
Steady Endurance__Z2__56-76.MIXED: Hold this pace smoothly. No surges, no coasting. Just consistent, controlled power that teaches your body to go the distance.

Base Work__Z2__55-70.DARK_HUMOR: Boring is fast. Don't get exciting here. Just ride steady and let your fitness grow quietly.
Base Work__Z2__55-70.MOTIVATIONAL: Base fitness is where it all starts. These steady efforts are building the aerobic capacity that makes everything else possible.
Base Work__Z2__55-70.TECHNICAL: Base-building phase uses extended Z2 work to increase mitochondrial density and metabolic efficiency without high fatigue accumulation.
Base Work__Z2__55-70.MIXED: This is sustainable effort. Hard enough to count, easy enough to do forever. Your foundation is getting stronger with every pedal stroke.

Sweet Spot__Z3__88-94.DARK_HUMOR: You're close enough to FTP to feel it, far enough away to do this repeatedly. This is where your fitness lives — the zone where suffering becomes productive.
Sweet Spot__Z3__88-94.MOTIVATIONAL: These efforts are building your engine. Every minute here teaches your body to hold harder power with less effort. This is real progress.
Sweet Spot__Z3__88-94.TECHNICAL: Sweet spot intensity hits the FTP sweet spot for training adaptations — high training stress with manageable fatigue, maximizing CTL gains per unit of fatigue.
Sweet Spot__Z3__88-94.MIXED: This is the productive pain zone. You're working but not maxing out. Stay focused, trust the effort, let your fitness grow.

Sweet Spot Block__Z3__85-95.DARK_HUMOR: You're in the sweet spot now. Not so easy you're joking, not so hard you're dying. This is precision training.
Sweet Spot Block__Z3__85-95.MOTIVATIONAL: You're building FTP at an incredibly efficient rate. This is where the magic happens — hard enough to stimulate adaptation, manageable enough to recover from.
Sweet Spot Block__Z3__85-95.TECHNICAL: Extended sweet spot efforts maximize training stress while maintaining sustainable lactate levels. Excellent for CTL development with controlled fatigue.
Sweet Spot Block__Z3__85-95.MIXED: Settle into this power. It's hard, yes, but sustainable. You're teaching your body to hold harder pace with less effort.

Tempo__Z3__76-90.DARK_HUMOR: This is the warm-up for threshold work — hard enough to get your attention, easy enough that you won't quit. The gateway drug to suffering.
Tempo__Z3__76-90.MOTIVATIONAL: You're building lactate clearing capacity here. These efforts teach your body to manage harder intensities gracefully. You're getting tougher mentally and physically.
Tempo__Z3__76-90.TECHNICAL: Sub-threshold tempo work stimulates lactate threshold adaptation and buffering capacity without the systemic fatigue of threshold efforts. Perfect for building work capacity.
Tempo__Z3__76-90.MIXED: Find a rhythm you can hold. Your legs will start to feel it around the 5-minute mark. That's when you know you're in the right zone.

Tempo Progression__Z3__76-95.DARK_HUMOR: Building tempo endurance, one hard minute at a time. Your lactate system is getting stronger with every second.
Tempo Progression__Z3__76-95.MOTIVATIONAL: You're progressing through tempo zones and getting stronger. Each step up teaches your body to handle harder efforts with control.
Tempo Progression__Z3__76-95.TECHNICAL: Progressive tempo work from Z3 into lower Z4 develops lactate threshold and buffering capacity systematically. Controlled intensity increase maximizes adaptation.
Tempo Progression__Z3__76-95.MIXED: Stay controlled as you progress. Your body is learning to handle harder power without falling apart. Trust the structure.

Threshold__Z4__95-105.DARK_HUMOR: Welcome to FTP. This is your redline in polite company. It hurts, you can't do it forever, and that's exactly the point.
Threshold__Z4__95-105.MOTIVATIONAL: This is where you get faster. Threshold work raises your ceiling — every interval here makes you a stronger, more capable cyclist. You're building your engine.
Threshold__Z4__95-105.TECHNICAL: FTP-intensity efforts maximally stimulate neuromuscular recruitment and VO2max without requiring total anaerobic capacity. The most time-efficient stimulus for power increase.
Threshold__Z4__95-105.MIXED: Hold this power steady. Your legs will argue with you somewhere in the middle — listen but don't give in. This is where the magic happens.

FTP Work__Z4__92-105.DARK_HUMOR: You're right at your limit here. This is where fitness happens and suffering lives in the same place.
FTP Work__Z4__92-105.MOTIVATIONAL: You're working at the power that defines your cycling engine. Every second here makes you faster, stronger, more capable.
FTP Work__Z4__92-105.TECHNICAL: FTP-level efforts drive maximal neuromuscular adaptation and lactate buffering. The most efficient intensity for raising your power ceiling.
FTP Work__Z4__92-105.MIXED: This is your limit in polite company. Stay focused. Your breathing should be controlled fury.

Threshold Repeats__Z4__95-105.DARK_HUMOR: Short FTP efforts with recovery. It's like threshold but you get to breathe between efforts. Living the dream.
Threshold Repeats__Z4__95-105.MOTIVATIONAL: These intervals are teaching your body to hold FTP power repeatedly. You're building muscular endurance at your limit.
Threshold Repeats__Z4__95-105.TECHNICAL: Threshold repeats allow higher volume at FTP with managed fatigue between efforts. Optimal for raising lactate threshold without excessive systemic stress.
Threshold Repeats__Z4__95-105.MIXED: Use the recovery to reset. Your body gets to recover slightly before the next effort. That's your tactical advantage here.

Long Threshold__Z4__90-105.DARK_HUMOR: Long FTP work. Your legs are going to have opinions about this for a while.
Long Threshold__Z4__90-105.MOTIVATIONAL: Extended threshold efforts build the endurance you need to hold hard power for the entire race. This is championship training.
Long Threshold__Z4__90-105.TECHNICAL: Sustained FTP work develops both lactate threshold power AND muscular endurance. Critical for lasting performance in races.
Long Threshold__Z4__90-105.MIXED: Pace yourself carefully here. You need to finish strong, not just survive. Control the beginning to own the end.

VO2 Max__Z5__106-120.DARK_HUMOR: Your aerobic system just got a nasty surprise. This is where lungs scream and legs burn simultaneously. Embrace the chaos.
VO2 Max__Z5__106-120.MOTIVATIONAL: You're reaching for your absolute aerobic potential here. Every breath is expanding your VO2max. This is the work of becoming an elite cyclist.
VO2 Max__Z5__106-120.TECHNICAL: VO2max intervals create maximal oxidative stress, driving mitochondrial biogenesis and capillary density. The most potent stimulus for aerobic power development.
VO2 Max__Z5__106-120.MIXED: Keep your cadence high — don't grind it. Yes, it's hard. Yes, that's the entire point. You're building something real here.

VO2 Repeats__Z5__110-125.DARK_HUMOR: VO2 intervals. You're training your lungs to handle maximum oxygen uptake. They will not enjoy this.
VO2 Repeats__Z5__110-125.MOTIVATIONAL: These are peak aerobic efforts. You're expanding your VO2max with every rep. This is elite fitness training.
VO2 Repeats__Z5__110-125.TECHNICAL: Supra-threshold VO2 efforts maximize EPOC and mitochondrial stimulus. Highest aerobic power development stimulus available.
VO2 Repeats__Z5__110-125.MIXED: Go hard here. Your lungs and legs are at capacity. That's exactly where you need to be.

VO2 Ladder__Z5__108-130.DARK_HUMOR: Ascending VO2. Getting harder with each step. Enjoy the early ones because the later ones will hurt.
VO2 Ladder__Z5__108-130.MOTIVATIONAL: You're climbing the VO2 ladder toward your aerobic ceiling. Each step up is making you stronger and more aerobically powerful.
VO2 Ladder__Z5__108-130.TECHNICAL: Progressive VO2 efforts from threshold up to maximum provide increasing oxidative stimulus. Develops both VO2max and lactate tolerance.
VO2 Ladder__Z5__108-130.MIXED: The ladder gets harder as you go up. Use the early efforts to warm your aerobic engine for the hard ones coming.

VO2 Short Repeats__Z5__110-130.DARK_HUMOR: Short hard VO2 work. Quick and brutal. You get to suffer and recover in rapid succession.
VO2 Short Repeats__Z5__110-130.MOTIVATIONAL: Short VO2 efforts teach your body to access peak aerobic power quickly and repeatedly. Race-winning capability right here.
VO2 Short Repeats__Z5__110-130.TECHNICAL: Shorter VO2 repeats (3-5min) maintain higher power while allowing adequate recovery. Optimal for volume at VO2max.
VO2 Short Repeats__Z5__110-130.MIXED: Go hard, recover hard, go hard again. Your aerobic system is learning to be powerful AND resilient.

Anaerobic__Z6__125-150.DARK_HUMOR: Your lactate levels just went nuclear. This is where your legs stop listening to your brain and start asking existential questions about life choices.
Anaerobic__Z6__125-150.MOTIVATIONAL: This is peak power work. You're tapping into your maximum effort capacity. These hard repeats teach your body and mind what you're capable of.
Anaerobic__Z6__125-150.TECHNICAL: Supra-threshold anaerobic work maximizes lactate production and tolerance while recruiting Type II fibers. Essential for breakaway and sprint power.
Anaerobic__Z6__125-150.MIXED: Go hard here — this is your time to attack. The recovery between efforts is your friend. Suffer now, celebrate later.

Anaerobic Repeats__Z6__130-160.DARK_HUMOR: Hard anaerobic work. Your legs are about to go to a dark place and come back stronger.
Anaerobic Repeats__Z6__130-160.MOTIVATIONAL: You're training at the edge of your anaerobic capacity. These efforts build the power needed to attack and drop competitors.
Anaerobic Repeats__Z6__130-160.TECHNICAL: Anaerobic repeats recruit maximum Type II fibers and tolerance lactate. Critical for race-winning breakaways.
Anaerobic Repeats__Z6__130-160.MIXED: These are all-out efforts. You're training your body to handle maximum sustained power. Go hard and earn the recovery.

Sprint__Z7__180-210.DARK_HUMOR: You're now riding like someone stole your bike. Maximum watts, maximum chaos, zero mercy. This is where cyclists find out what they're really made of.
Sprint__Z7__180-210.MOTIVATIONAL: All-out maximum effort. You're discovering your absolute power ceiling. These sprints build explosive strength that makes you dangerous in any race situation.
Sprint__Z7__180-210.TECHNICAL: Maximal power efforts recruit complete neuromuscular system and generate peak force output. Critical for race-winning moves and tactical acceleration.
Sprint__Z7__180-210.MIXED: Give it everything you have. This is your moment. The recovery after is earned — you've already succeeded.

Sprint Repeats__Z7__190-220.DARK_HUMOR: Max effort sprints. Your glycolytic system is about to get a serious wake-up call.
Sprint Repeats__Z7__190-220.MOTIVATIONAL: You're training peak power and sprint capacity. These efforts will make you dangerous in any finish or attack situation.
Sprint Repeats__Z7__190-220.TECHNICAL: Maximum power sprints recruit Type IIx fibers and max neuromuscular output. Essential for race tactics and finishes.
Sprint Repeats__Z7__190-220.MIXED: All-out for the duration. You're teaching your body what maximum power feels like and how to access it under fatigue.

Cooldown__Z1__30-50.DARK_HUMOR: You survived. Now your job is to convince your legs that the suffering is over. Easy spinning, deep breathing, gratitude for not being dead.
Cooldown__Z1__30-50.MOTIVATIONAL: Great work! You earned this easy spin. Let your body transition gracefully from hard effort back to recovery. You should feel proud.
Cooldown__Z1__30-50.TECHNICAL: Low-intensity spinning promotes active recovery, lactate clearance, and parasympathetic nervous system activation. Improves overall recovery trajectory.
Cooldown__Z1__30-50.MIXED: Breathe easy now. Spin the legs out, let your heart rate come down, let the victory sink in. You did good work today.

Cool-down__Z1__35-55.DARK_HUMOR: The suffering is over. Now just spin easy and let your body remember what normal feels like.
Cool-down__Z1__35-55.MOTIVATIONAL: You earned this recovery spin. Let your breathing normalize and your muscles transition from work to rest.
Cool-down__Z1__35-55.TECHNICAL: Cool-down Z1 work activates recovery pathways and begins lactate clearance. Gentle transition from exercise to baseline.
Cool-down__Z1__35-55.MIXED: Easy spinning. Your workout is done. Just let yourself recover gracefully.

Technique__Z2__60-80.DARK_HUMOR: Time to remember how to pedal smoothly instead of like a washing machine. Your form coach is about to earn their paycheck.
Technique__Z2__60-80.MOTIVATIONAL: Economy of motion = speed for free. These skill drills are teaching your body to work smarter. Small improvements in technique compound into real gains.
Technique__Z2__60-80.TECHNICAL: Pedaling efficiency work activates neuromuscular proprioception and optimizes force application across the pedal stroke. Increases wattage without additional fatigue.
Technique__Z2__60-80.MIXED: Focus on fluidity here. Smooth pedal strokes, consistent cadence, no dead spots. This is meditation on a bike.

Pedaling Drill__Z2__60-75.DARK_HUMOR: Pedaling drill time. Your technique is about to get a lesson in how to actually pedal.
Pedaling Drill__Z2__60-75.MOTIVATIONAL: Smooth pedaling is fast pedaling. You're training your neuromuscular system to be efficient. This builds real speed.
Pedaling Drill__Z2__60-75.TECHNICAL: Isolated pedaling drills activate neuromuscular efficiency and optimal power delivery. Improves economy at all intensities.
Pedaling Drill__Z2__60-75.MIXED: Focus on smooth, circular pedaling. No grinding, no rushing. Your body is learning efficiency.

Strength__Z2__70-90.DARK_HUMOR: Big gear, low cadence. Your muscles don't like this, which is exactly why your muscles are about to get a lot stronger. You're building horsepower.
Strength__Z2__70-90.MOTIVATIONAL: Strength work builds the power foundation everything else relies on. Every heavy pedal stroke here makes you more capable at any intensity.
Strength__Z2__70-90.TECHNICAL: Low-cadence force work maximizes mechanical advantage and Type II fiber recruitment without aerobic stress. Critical for building absolute power capacity.
Strength__Z2__70-90.MIXED: Stay at 50-60 RPM, stay smooth. Your legs will want to spin faster — resist. The effort is real, but the form stays clean.

Strength Work__Z2__65-85.DARK_HUMOR: Big gear, heavy legs. You're building raw power here, not speed.
Strength Work__Z2__65-85.MOTIVATIONAL: Every rotation in this big gear makes your muscles stronger and more powerful. You're building the foundation for everything.
Strength Work__Z2__65-85.TECHNICAL: Heavy resistance low-cadence work recruits maximum muscle fibers for force development. Builds absolute power capacity.
Strength Work__Z2__65-85.MIXED: Stay controlled in the big gear. Strength isn't about speed here — it's about force. Trust the resistance.

Low Cadence__Z2__70-85.DARK_HUMOR: Your legs are now rocks. Slow, heavy, powerful rocks.
Low Cadence__Z2__70-85.MOTIVATIONAL: Low cadence work teaches your legs to generate massive force. You're building the power that makes harder intensities possible.
Low Cadence__Z2__70-85.TECHNICAL: Sub-80 RPM cadence maximizes mechanical advantage and Type II fiber recruitment. Optimal for strength development.
Low Cadence__Z2__70-85.MIXED: Keep it slow and powerful. Your cadence might want to creep up — resist. The strength happens at 50-60 RPM.

Over-Under__Z4-Z5__95-115.DARK_HUMOR: Over FTP, under FTP, over again. You're teaching your lactate system to handle wildly changing demands.
Over-Under__Z4-Z5__95-115.MOTIVATIONAL: Over-under efforts train your body to handle rapid intensity changes. This teaches you how to respond to race attacks.
Over-Under__Z4-Z5__95-115.TECHNICAL: Over-under intervals develop lactate clearance and threshold tolerance by forcing rapid steady-state adjustments. Race-specific stimulus.
Over-Under__Z4-Z5__95-115.MIXED: You're riding above and below your limit. Your body learns to adapt quickly. This is race-prep training.

VO2 Over-Under__Z5-Z6__110-130.DARK_HUMOR: Max VO2 over-under. Your aerobic system is about to learn some harsh lessons about adapting to chaos.
VO2 Over-Under__Z5-Z6__110-130.MOTIVATIONAL: These intervals teach your aerobic system to handle maximum intensity variations. You're training race-winning adaptability.
VO2 Over-Under__Z5-Z6__110-130.TECHNICAL: High-intensity over-under develops maximum VO2 with lactate management. Elite-level training stimulus.
VO2 Over-Under__Z5-Z6__110-130.MIXED: Go hard over FTP, control under. Your body learns to access peak power even when fatigued.

Microbursts__Z2-Z5__60-130.DARK_HUMOR: Short power surges in easy effort. Your legs don't see it coming. That's the point.
Microbursts__Z2-Z5__60-130.MOTIVATIONAL: Microbursts teach your body to access power instantly. You're training race-awareness and tactical response.
Microbursts__Z2-Z5__60-130.TECHNICAL: Brief neuromuscular spikes within aerobic base develop power without fatigue accumulation. Excellent for maintaining race-readiness.
Microbursts__Z2-Z5__60-130.MIXED: Easy base work with surprise power efforts. Your body learns to be ready for anything.

Mixed Efforts__Z3-Z5__85-125.DARK_HUMOR: Your intensity is all over the place. That's exactly what your aerobic system needed to learn.
Mixed Efforts__Z3-Z5__85-125.MOTIVATIONAL: Mixed-intensity work teaches your body to handle variety. You're training adaptability and resilience.
Mixed Efforts__Z3-Z5__85-125.TECHNICAL: Varied-zone efforts develop broad training adaptations and prevent monotony. Efficient for general fitness.
Mixed Efforts__Z3-Z5__85-125.MIXED: You're working at different intensities. Your body is learning to be powerful across the spectrum.

Alternating Intensity__Z3-Z6__80-140.DARK_HUMOR: Easy, hard, easy, hard. Your energy systems are getting a complete workout in rapid succession.
Alternating Intensity__Z3-Z6__80-140.MOTIVATIONAL: You're training rapid intensity transitions. This teaches your body to be resilient and powerful in any situation.
Alternating Intensity__Z3-Z6__80-140.TECHNICAL: Rapid intensity alternation develops lactate buffering and energy system flexibility. Excellent for race-specific adaptations.
Alternating Intensity__Z3-Z6__80-140.MIXED: You're working through all your zones. Your entire energy system is getting trained.

Pyramid__Z4-Z5__95-125.DARK_HUMOR: Climbing the pyramid, then descending it. You get to suffer going up AND going down.
Pyramid__Z4-Z5__95-125.MOTIVATIONAL: Pyramid efforts teach pacing and power management. You're learning to ride smart and strong.
Pyramid__Z4-Z5__95-125.TECHNICAL: Progressive-then-descending efforts develop lactate tolerance and pacing awareness. Improves race performance.
Pyramid__Z4-Z5__95-125.MIXED: Climb steady, descend with control. Your body learns power management across fatigue.

Descending Intervals__Z4-Z5__105-125.DARK_HUMOR: Getting harder with each effort. Your legs are learning what progressive suffering feels like.
Descending Intervals__Z4-Z5__105-125.MOTIVATIONAL: You're riding harder as you fatigue. This teaches you mental toughness and power under duress.
Descending Intervals__Z4-Z5__105-125.TECHNICAL: Descending (increasing) intensity develops lactate tolerance and mental resilience under fatigue. Race simulation.
Descending Intervals__Z4-Z5__105-125.MIXED: Each repeat gets harder. You're training yourself to keep power even when tired.

Ladder__Z4-Z5__95-130.DARK_HUMOR: Ascending ladder. Each rung is harder than the last. Enjoy the bottom because the top hurts.
Ladder__Z4-Z5__95-130.MOTIVATIONAL: You're climbing toward your peak power. Each step builds on the last. This is progressive strength training.
Ladder__Z4-Z5__95-130.TECHNICAL: Progressive intensity increases develop power across multiple energy systems. Excellent for comprehensive fitness.
Ladder__Z4-Z5__95-130.MIXED: Each rung climbs higher. Stay controlled early so you have power for the top.

Ramp Test__Z1-Z6__35-200.DARK_HUMOR: Your FTP assessment. Time to find out where your real limit is. Spoiler: it hurts more than you think.
Ramp Test__Z1-Z6__35-200.MOTIVATIONAL: This is your moment to discover your power. You're testing your absolute capability. Own it.
Ramp Test__Z1-Z6__35-200.TECHNICAL: Ramp protocol establishes FTP through progressive power increase until failure. Gold standard for FTP assessment.
Ramp Test__Z1-Z6__35-200.MIXED: You're finding your limit. Go until you can't. That number defines your engine.

FTP Test__Z4__92-100.DARK_HUMOR: 20 minutes at your absolute limit. You can't phone this in. Your body knows.
FTP Test__Z4__92-100.MOTIVATIONAL: This is your measure. 20 minutes of best effort reveals your true power. You've got this.
FTP Test__Z4__92-100.TECHNICAL: 20-minute max effort establishes FTP as 95% of 20-min power. Accurate real-world threshold measurement.
FTP Test__Z4__92-100.MIXED: You get 20 minutes. Make them count. Your fitness is being defined right now.

Extended FTP Test__Z4__90-105.DARK_HUMOR: Long FTP test. More than 20 minutes of your life at threshold. You're going to remember this.
Extended FTP Test__Z4__90-105.MOTIVATIONAL: You're testing sustained power. This reveals not just your FTP, but your muscular endurance at threshold.
Extended FTP Test__Z4__90-105.TECHNICAL: Extended threshold testing (30-40min) measures sustained FTP power and muscular endurance. Race-specific assessment.
Extended FTP Test__Z4__90-105.MIXED: Settle in. You need to be strong for the whole duration. Pace smart.

Race Simulation__Z3-Z6__80-150.DARK_HUMOR: Race simulation. Your body is about to experience what the actual race will feel like. Fun times.
Race Simulation__Z3-Z6__80-150.MOTIVATIONAL: You're training for race day. This prepares your body and mind for real competition. You've got this.
Race Simulation__Z3-Z6__80-150.TECHNICAL: Race-pace efforts with varied intensity develop race-specific fitness. Excellent pre-race preparation.
Race Simulation__Z3-Z6__80-150.MIXED: This is what race day feels like. Your body is learning what to expect.

Breakaway Prep__Z5-Z6__120-150.DARK_HUMOR: Training to attack and drop people. Your legs are going to be weapons after this.
Breakaway Prep__Z5-Z6__120-150.MOTIVATIONAL: You're training to break away and win. This is champion-level work.
Breakaway Prep__Z5-Z6__120-150.TECHNICAL: Supra-threshold repeat work develops the power needed for race-winning breakaways. Anaerobic capacity building.
Breakaway Prep__Z5-Z6__120-150.MIXED: This is tactical power. You're training to attack when it matters.

Cadence Drill__Z2__60-80.DARK_HUMOR: Cadence variation training. Your legs are learning different rhythms.
Cadence Drill__Z2__60-80.MOTIVATIONAL: You're developing cadence flexibility. High cadence, low cadence, smooth transitions. You're becoming a complete cyclist.
Cadence Drill__Z2__60-80.TECHNICAL: Cadence variation improves neuromuscular adaptability across RPM ranges. Develops pedaling versatility.
Cadence Drill__Z2__60-80.MIXED: Work through different cadences. Your body learns to be powerful at any rhythm.

Standing Effort__Z4-Z5__100-130.DARK_HUMOR: Out of the saddle power. You're about to remember why sitting is comfortable.
Standing Effort__Z4-Z5__100-130.MOTIVATIONAL: Standing work teaches explosive power and tactical acceleration. You're training weapon skills.
Standing Effort__Z4-Z5__100-130.TECHNICAL: Out-of-saddle work recruits different muscle groups and generates max power. Essential for climbs and attacks.
Standing Effort__Z4-Z5__100-130.MIXED: Go hard out of the saddle. Your whole body is engaged. Control it and use that power.

Seated Effort__Z4-Z5__95-120.DARK_HUMOR: Seated power work. You're generating force without leaving the saddle.
Seated Effort__Z4-Z5__95-120.MOTIVATIONAL: Seated efforts teach sustainable power management. This is the foundation of steady racing.
Seated Effort__Z4-Z5__95-120.TECHNICAL: Seated work develops sustainable power at threshold without neuromuscular spike. Efficient for sustained efforts.
Seated Effort__Z4-Z5__95-120.MIXED: Stay smooth in the saddle. Power comes from control, not violence.

Jumps__Z6-Z7__150-210.DARK_HUMOR: Jump intervals. Your legs are about to experience explosive acceleration. Multiple times.
Jumps__Z6-Z7__150-210.MOTIVATIONAL: You're training the power to attack. Jump intervals build your ability to surprise and drop competitors.
Jumps__Z6-Z7__150-210.TECHNICAL: Jump efforts develop neuromuscular explosiveness and max power output. Critical for tactical acceleration.
Jumps__Z6-Z7__150-210.MIXED: Each jump is all-out. Your body is learning instant power access.

Base Phase Work__Z2__55-70.DARK_HUMOR: Base building. Boring is where fitness hides.
Base Phase Work__Z2__55-70.MOTIVATIONAL: You're laying the foundation for everything else. Base fitness is the most important fitness.
Base Phase Work__Z2__55-70.TECHNICAL: Base phase develops aerobic capacity and fat oxidation. Foundation for all higher intensities.
Base Phase Work__Z2__55-70.MIXED: Steady, consistent work. Your fitness is growing quietly and powerfully.

Build Phase Work__Z3-Z4__88-105.DARK_HUMOR: Build phase. Your training is getting serious now. The real work starts.
Build Phase Work__Z3-Z4__88-105.MOTIVATIONAL: You're building toward your peak. Every session now is moving you closer to your best.
Build Phase Work__Z3-Z4__88-105.TECHNICAL: Build phase emphasizes sweet spot and threshold. Develops FTP and muscular endurance.
Build Phase Work__Z3-Z4__88-105.MIXED: You're ramping up the intensity. Your fitness is building toward something real.

Peak Phase Work__Z5-Z6__110-150.DARK_HUMOR: Peak phase. You're training at your absolute limit. This is the final push before tapering.
Peak Phase Work__Z5-Z6__110-150.MOTIVATIONAL: This is your peak training. You're at your best right now. Race soon.
Peak Phase Work__Z5-Z6__110-150.TECHNICAL: Peak phase emphasizes VO2max and anaerobic power. Maximum fitness expression right before racing.
Peak Phase Work__Z5-Z6__110-150.MIXED: You're at your sharpest. This is what you've trained for. Use it now.

Recovery Phase__Z1-Z2__40-75.DARK_HUMOR: Recovery phase. Your job is to not break yourself. Easy riding, light effort, lots of sleep.
Recovery Phase__Z1-Z2__40-75.MOTIVATIONAL: You've earned recovery. Your body is adapting and growing stronger. Rest is part of training.
Recovery Phase__Z1-Z2__40-75.TECHNICAL: Recovery phase uses low-intensity work to promote adaptation while minimizing fatigue. Essential for long-term progression.
Recovery Phase__Z1-Z2__40-75.MIXED: Take it easy. Your body has done incredible work. Now let it recover and grow.

Deload Week__Z1-Z2__40-65.DARK_HUMOR: Deload week. Your body is getting permission to rest. Don't waste it. Just spin easy.
Deload Week__Z1-Z2__40-65.MOTIVATIONAL: This is recovery and adaptation time. Your body grows when you rest, not when you train. Enjoy this.
Deload Week__Z1-Z2__40-65.TECHNICAL: Deload weeks reduce fatigue and allow nervous system recovery. Essential for long-term performance.
Deload Week__Z1-Z2__40-65.MIXED: Keep it easy. You've earned this break. Just spin and recover.
